metadata.title: Context-Dependent Expression Persistence: AR(2) Eigenvalue |λ| Is Independent of Intrinsic mRNA Half-Life
metadata.author: Michael Whiteside

exemplar_genes.explanation: Sustained interferon-driven retranscription via JAK-STAT-IRF pathway produces high temporal autocorrelation despite rapid mRNA turnover
Fast immune responder with rapid mRNA decay but sustained NF-κB-driven transcription during inflammation
Immediate-early gene with transient expression pulse; rapid induction followed by quick decay
Ribosomal protein with stable transcript but constitutive, non-dynamic expression pattern
Housekeeping gene with long-lived transcript but minimal temporal structure in expression

irrelevant_metrics.result: PASS — no meaningful correlation with static gene properties

expression_matched_null.result: PASS — high persistence is not an artifact of expression level

non_circadian_replication.description: Extended half-life independence test to 3 additional non-circadian datasets (immune response, cancer)

datasets.notes: 7 timepoints is marginal for AR(2); eigenvalues survive time-shuffling indicating fits capture static properties not temporal dynamics; apparent correlation driven entirely by explosive (|λ|>1) genes
Marginal significance; CI lower bound barely above zero (0.011); 9 timepoints provides slightly better AR(2) fits than Rabani
Apparent correlation (ρ=0.20) drops to ρ=0.045 after controlling for expression level; confounded by highly-expressed housekeeping genes having both long half-lives and stable AR(2) fits

sample_size.detail: New datasets have 30-70x fewer matched genes than Paper F (85-190 vs 5,945); all underpowered to detect ρ=0.1 at 80% power (need n≈388)

timepoint_count.detail: 7-14 timepoints vs 48 in Paper F; AR(2) requires minimum 5 residuals; 7 timepoints is marginal

halflife_source_mismatch.detail: Sharova half-lives from mouse ESCs applied to dendritic cells and human neuroblastoma; half-lives can vary 2-3x across cell types

gene_selection_bias.detail: Only 21 unique half-life values across 85 genes; biased toward well-studied immune, housekeeping, and signaling genes

explosive_dynamics.detail: 75/453 matched genes have |λ|>1; removing these eliminates correlation in Rabani (ρ → -0.04)

non_circadian_replication.qualified_conclusion: AR(2) eigenvalue is independent of mRNA half-life in datasets with adequate temporal resolution (≥24 timepoints, n>1000 matched genes). In short non-circadian time-series (<15 timepoints), weak apparent correlations (ρ=0.13-0.20) arise from explosive dynamics and expression-level confounding; these dissolve after controlling for explosive eigenvalues (ρ→-0.04) or expression level (ρ→0.045).

key_conclusion: The AR(2) eigenvalue modulus |λ| measures context-dependent regulatory persistence arising from transcription factor dynamics, feedback loops, and signaling cascades—not intrinsic mRNA stability. The near-zero correlation with mRNA half-life (ρ = 0.006), validated across 4 datasets spanning 3 species and replicated across 3 additional non-circadian datasets (total: 7 datasets, 22,989 genes, 4 species, 6 biological contexts), establishes |λ| as a novel axis of gene expression variation independent of all previously characterized transcript properties. Weak apparent correlations in short time-series dissolve after controlling for explosive dynamics and expression level.
